overview: Converts characters in text units between full-width (zenkaku) and half-width (hankaku) forms, targeting Asian character sets that historically have dual display forms. The conversion operates on the target locale text of text units; if no target text exists, source text is copied to the target before processing. Supports both directions: full-width → half-width/ASCII and half-width/ASCII → full-width, with fine-grained control over which character classes are affected.

toHalfWidth.help:
Controls the conversion direction.

- **true** (default): Converts full-width characters to half-width or ASCII equivalents. E.g., `Ｑ` (U+FF31) → `Q` (U+0051), `サ` (U+30B5) → `ｻ` (U+FF7B).
- **false**: Converts half-width and ASCII characters to full-width equivalents. E.g., `Q` (U+0051) → `Ｑ` (U+FF31), `ｻ` (U+FF7B) → `サ` (U+30B5).

The `asciiOnly`, `katakanaOnly`, `includeSLA`, `includeLLS`, and `includeKatakana` options modify behavior differently depending on this direction setting.
examples: `true` — Normalize Japanese text by converting full-width alphanumerics to standard ASCII
`false` — Convert ASCII characters to full-width for CJK-style typesetting

asciiOnly.help:
When converting **to full-width** (`toHalfWidth` = false), restricts conversion to ASCII characters only. Half-width Katakana and other half-width characters are left unchanged.

Useful when you want full-width alphanumeric rendering but need to preserve half-width Katakana as-is.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is false (converting to full-width)

katakanaOnly.help:
When converting **to full-width** (`toHalfWidth` = false), restricts conversion to Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`｡､｢｣`, etc.) only. Alphanumeric characters remain half-width.

Useful for normalizing Japanese text where half-width Katakana should become full-width but ASCII/alphanumeric characters should stay as-is.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is false (converting to full-width)

includeSLA.help:
When converting **to half-width** (`toHalfWidth` = true), also converts Squared Latin Abbreviations from the CJK Compatibility block (U+3300–U+33FF) into sequences of non-CJK characters.

Example: `㏀` (U+33C0) → `kΩ` (U+006B, U+03A9).

These are additional conversions beyond the standard full-width → half-width mapping.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

includeLLS.help:
When converting **to half-width** (`toHalfWidth` = true), also converts several characters from the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences:

| Symbol | Result |
|--------|--------|
| U+2100 | `a/c` |
| U+2101 | `a/s` |
| U+2105 | `c/o` |
| U+2103 | `°C` |
| U+2109 | `°F` |
| U+2116 | `No` |
| U+212A | `K` |
| U+212B | `Å` |

These are additional conversions beyond the standard full-width → half-width mapping.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

includeKatakana.help:
When converting **to half-width** (`toHalfWidth` = true), also converts Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`。、「」`, etc.) into their half-width forms.

Off by default to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: typically full-width alphanumerics should be normalized to half-width ASCII, while Katakana should remain full-width.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

notes: Off by default because in modern Japanese text normalization, Katakana is typically kept full-width while only alphanumeric characters are converted to half-width.

normalizeOutput.help:
Applies Unicode NFC normalization to the output text after conversions are made.

Converting half-width Katakana to full-width can produce decomposed character sequences. For example, `ﾌﾟ` (U+FF8C U+FF9F) converts to `プ` (U+30D5 U+309A, decomposed), but NFC normalization produces the standard precomposed form `プ` (U+30D7).

Enabled by default to ensure consistent Unicode representation.
notes: Only applied when at least one conversion is made. If no characters are converted, the text passes through unchanged.

toHalfWidth.title: Convert to Half-Width
toHalfWidth.description: Convert full-width characters to half-width or ASCII equivalents; when false, converts in the opposite direction.

asciiOnly.title: ASCII Only
asciiOnly.description: When converting to full-width, restrict conversion to ASCII characters only.

katakanaOnly.title: Katakana Only
katakanaOnly.description: When converting to full-width, restrict conversion to Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation only.

includeSLA.title: Include Squared Latin Abbreviations
includeSLA.description: Also convert Squared Latin Abbreviations from the CJK Compatibility block to non-CJK character sequences.

includeLLS.title: Include Letter-Like Symbols
includeLLS.description: Also convert characters from the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences.

includeKatakana.title: Include Katakana
includeKatakana.description: Also convert Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation to half-width forms.

normalizeOutput.title: Normalize Output (NFC)
normalizeOutput.description: Apply Unicode NFC normalization to the output text after conversions.

processingNotes: Operates on the target locale text of text units. If no target text exists, the source text is copied to the target before processing.
The step handles two distinct conversion directions controlled by `toHalfWidth`, each with its own set of sub-options.
Unicode NFC normalization (when enabled) only runs if at least one character conversion was made.

examples.title: Normalize Japanese full-width alphanumerics to half-width
examples.description: Convert full-width ASCII-range characters to standard half-width while keeping Katakana full-width — a common normalization for modern Japanese text.
examples.config:
```yaml
toHalfWidth: true
includeKatakana: false
normalizeOutput: true
```

examples.title: Convert ASCII to full-width for CJK typesetting
examples.description: Convert ASCII characters to full-width equivalents while leaving Katakana unchanged.

examples.title: Normalize half-width Katakana to full-width
examples.description: Convert half-width Katakana to full-width while keeping alphanumerics as-is, with NFC normalization for consistent output.
examples.config:
```yaml
toHalfWidth: false
katakanaOnly: true
normalizeOutput: true
```

fullDoc:
# Full-Width Conversion Step

Converts characters in text units between full-width (zenkaku) and half-width (hankaku) forms, targeting Asian character sets that historically have dual display forms. The conversion operates on the target locale text of text units; if no target text exists, source text is copied to the target before processing. Supports both directions: full-width → half-width/ASCII and half-width/ASCII → full-width, with fine-grained control over which character classes are affected.

**Takes:** Filter events. **Sends:** Filter events.

## Parameters

### Conversion Direction

#### Convert to Half-Width

Controls the conversion direction.

- **true** (default): Converts full-width characters to half-width or ASCII equivalents. E.g., `Ｑ` (U+FF31) → `Q` (U+0051), `サ` (U+30B5) → `ｻ` (U+FF7B).
- **false**: Converts half-width and ASCII characters to full-width equivalents. E.g., `Q` (U+0051) → `Ｑ` (U+FF31), `ｻ` (U+FF7B) → `サ` (U+30B5).

### Half-Width Options (toHalfWidth = true)

#### Include Squared Latin Abbreviations

Also converts Squared Latin Abbreviations from the CJK Compatibility block (U+3300–U+33FF) into sequences of non-CJK characters. Example: `㏀` (U+33C0) → `kΩ`.

#### Include Letter-Like Symbols

Also converts several characters from the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences:

| Symbol | Result |
|--------|--------|
| U+2100 | `a/c` |
| U+2101 | `a/s` |
| U+2105 | `c/o` |
| U+2103 | `°C` |
| U+2109 | `°F` |
| U+2116 | `No` |
| U+212A | `K` |
| U+212B | `Å` |

#### Include Katakana

Also converts Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`。、「」`, etc.) into their half-width forms. Off by default because in modern Japanese text normalization, Katakana is typically kept full-width while only alphanumeric characters are converted.

> Available since 0.29-SNAPSHOT.

### Full-Width Options (toHalfWidth = false)

#### ASCII Only

Restricts conversion to ASCII characters only. Half-width Katakana and other half-width characters are left unchanged.

#### Katakana Only

Restricts conversion to Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`｡､｢｣`, etc.) only. Alphanumeric characters remain half-width.

> Available since 0.29-SNAPSHOT.

### Output

#### Normalize Output (NFC)

Applies Unicode NFC normalization to the output text after conversions. Converting half-width Katakana to full-width can produce decomposed character sequences — for example, `ﾌﾟ` (U+FF8C U+FF9F) → `プ` (U+30D5 U+309A, decomposed). NFC normalization produces the standard precomposed form `プ` (U+30D7). Enabled by default.

> Available since 0.29-SNAPSHOT.

## Limitations

None known.

## Notes

- Operates on the target locale text of text units. If no target text exists, the source text is copied to the target before processing.
- The step handles two distinct conversion directions controlled by `toHalfWidth`, each with its own set of sub-options: `includeSLA`/`includeLLS`/`includeKatakana` apply to half-width conversion, while `asciiOnly`/`katakanaOnly` apply to full-width conversion.

## Examples

### Normalize Japanese full-width alphanumerics to half-width

Convert full-width ASCII-range characters to standard half-width while keeping Katakana full-width:

```yaml
toHalfWidth: true
includeKatakana: false
normalizeOutput: true
```

Input: `Ｈｅｌｌｏ サンプル １２３` → Output: `Hello サンプル 123`

### Convert ASCII to full-width for CJK typesetting

```yaml
toHalfWidth: false
asciiOnly: true
```

Input: `Hello 123` → Output: `Ｈｅｌｌｏ １２３`

### Normalize half-width Katakana to full-width

```yaml
toHalfWidth: false
katakanaOnly: true
normalizeOutput: true
```

Input: `Hello ｻﾝﾌﾟﾙ 123` → Output: `Hello サンプル 123`
